etymology.description: 針(針) (zhēn, "needle") and 灸(灸) (jiǔ, "moxibustion") combine to describe the practice of acupuncture, which involves using needles and heat to stimulate specific points on the body for therapeutic purposes.
etymology.interpretation: 針(針) (zhēn, "needle") refers to the slender pointed tool used in acupuncture, while 灸(灸) (jiǔ, "moxibustion") involves the burning of moxa (a plant material) to create therapeutic heat. Together, 針灸(針灸) (zhēnjiǔ) denotes the combined use of both these methods to apply physical stimulation and heat to specific points on the body, based on traditional Chinese medicine principles to balance and regulate the flow of qi (vital energy).

examples.chinese: 他对针灸疗法(他對針灸療法)很感兴趣。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì zhēnjiǔ liáofǎ hěn gǎn xìngqù.
examples.english: He is very interested in acupuncture therapy.

examples.chinese: 针灸可以缓解疼痛(針灸可以緩解疼痛)。
医生建议我尝试针灸疗法(醫生建議我嘗試針灸療法)，因为效果显著(因為效果顯著)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng jiànyì wǒ chángshì zhēnjiǔ liáofǎ, yīnwèi xiàoguǒ xiǎnzhù.
examples.english: The doctor suggested I try acupuncture therapy because the effects are remarkable.
